title: Source Reconciliation — AIS vs 26AS
description: For each income category and TDS entry, compare the Annual Information Statement (AIS, Section 285BB) against Form 26AS (Section 203AA). Compute the variance as a percentage of the higher-authority value. Classify the reconciliation status and produce a conflict entry for any discrepancy above the auto-resolve threshold. 26AS is the higher-authority source for TDS credit amounts; AIS is the higher-authority source for income amounts.

logic.if: AIS is loaded (E-001) AND 26AS is loaded (E-002)
logic.then: for each income_category in {salary, interest, dividend, business_receipts, capital_gains, rent}: locate matching entry in both AIS and 26AS; compute delta = |AIS.amount - 26AS.amount| / max(AIS.amount, 26AS.amount); apply threshold_table: delta < 0.01 → reconciled (use 26AS value for TDS, AIS value for income); 0.01 ≤ delta ≤ 0.05 → minor_variance (warn, proceed); delta > 0.05 → major_variance (conflict, add to conflict_log, reduce ECS); entry in AIS only → presence_mismatch (trigger V-002 candidate); entry in 26AS only → presence_mismatch (investigate: may be TDS on exempt income)
logic.else: if AIS not loaded: mark all AIS-sourced income fields as missing_source (DS=0 in ECS); if 26AS not loaded: mark all TDS credits as unverified (PU=0.5 in ECS); do not proceed with TDS claim computation until 26AS is loaded

notes: AIS and 26AS are both sourced from the Income Tax Department but maintained by different sub-systems. Small discrepancies (< 1%) are common due to timing differences in TDS deposit and AIS update cycles. Discrepancies > 5% almost always indicate either a genuine underpayment by the employer or a data entry error in AIS — both require the taxpayer to resolve before filing. Authority hierarchy: 26AS > AIS for TDS credit claims (only 26AS TDS is claimable as credit). AIS > 26AS for income amounts (AIS has broader coverage of income sources).
